name: Dummy Deck
description: Sandbox for testing new technical phrases before migration.

cards.sideA: Si tenemos el valor de A, entonces multiplicamos con el valor de B.

sideB.text: If we have the value of A, then we multiply it by the value of B.
If we get A's value, we then multiply it by B's value.
If we have A, then we multiply by B.
If we have the value A, then we multiply it by the value B.
If we have the value of A, we multiply it by the value of B.
If we have the A value, we multiply it by the value of B.

cards.sideA: Cuando la linea es de retorno multiplicamos por la cantidad en negativo.

sideB.text: When it's a return line, we multiply by the negative amount.
When the line is a return, we multiply by the negative quantity.
If it's a return line, we multiply by the amount in negative.
When it's a return line, we multiply by the negative quantity.
When it's a return line, we multiply it by a negative amount.

cards.note:
**[94]**

~When the line is return, we multiply by the negative amount~ is not valid because [return] acts as a noun here, so it requires an article like [a return] or must be used as an adjective modifying a noun, like [a return line].

~When it's a return line, we multiply it by the amount in negative~ is not valid because 'in negative' is a literal translation of 'en negativo'. In English, the adjective precedes the noun, so use [the negative amount] or [a negative amount].

cards.sideA: Y si no es de retorno, multiplicamos por 1 positivo, para mantener el valor original.

sideB.text: And if it's not a return, we multiply by positive one to keep the original value.
And if it isn't a return, we multiply by plus one to maintain the original value.
And if it's not a return line, we multiply by a positive one to keep the original amount.
And if it's not a return, we multiply it by positive one to keep the original value.

cards.note: ~And if it's not returned, we multiply by positive one to keep the original value.~ is not valid because 'returned' is an action (devuelto), whereas the original sentence refers to a type or category (de retorno). Use [a return].

sideB.text: What's outside the simulation?
What is outside of the simulation?
What lies beyond the simulation?
What is there outside the simulation?

cards.note: While [there is] translates to "hay", when asking about the identity or nature of what exists in a location, English frequently uses [What is...] directly as the subject. [What is outside?] is more concise and natural than [What is there outside?], though both are grammatically valid.

cards.sideA: Tuve que aprender a hacerme entender con diferente tipo de gente.

sideB.text: I had to learn to make myself understood with different types of people.
I had to learn how to get my point across to different kinds of people.
I needed to learn to make myself clear to different types of people.
I had to learn how to make myself understood with different types of people.

cards.note:
**[18]**

~I had to learn to treat different kind of people~ changes the original meaning of "hacerme entender" (make myself understood) to "lidiar/tratar con". Additionally, [treat] means to behave towards someone in a certain way or give medical care, whereas to interact with or manage people is [deal with]. Finally, it should be [different kinds] (plural).

~I had to learn to make myself understood with different kind of people~ is not valid because 'kind' must be plural when preceded by 'different' referring to multiple types. Use [different kinds of people].

cards.sideA: A causa de los espacios algunos caracteres invisibles son agregados.

sideB.text: Because of the spaces, some invisible characters are added.
Due to the spaces, some invisible characters get added.
Some invisible characters are added because of the spaces.
Due to spaces, some invisible characters get added.
Because of spacing, some invisible characters are added.

cards.note:
**[212]**

~Because of some spaces, some invisible characters are added~ changes the meaning slightly from "los espacios" (definite) to "algunos espacios" (indefinite). Use [the spaces].

~Because of spaces, some invisible characters are added~ changes the meaning from specific spaces ([the spaces]) to spaces in general. Since the original Spanish says "los espacios", use [the spaces].

cards.sideA: El cliente devolvió el producto defectuoso a la tienda.

sideB.text: The customer returned the defective product to the store.
The customer brought the faulty product back to the store.
The customer took the broken item back to the shop.

cards.note:
**[210]**

Use [customer] when referring to someone buying a physical product in a retail transaction. Do not use ~client~ here.

~The customer returned the failed product to the store.~ is not valid because 'failed' usually refers to a lack of success rather than a physical defect. Use [defective], [faulty], or [broken] for physical items.

cards.sideA: Si dejas el campo en blanco, la validación fallará.

sideB.text: If you leave the field blank, the validation will fail.
The validation will fail if you leave the input blank.
If that field is left blank, it will fail the validation.
If you leave a blank field, the validation will fail.

cards.note:
**[210]**

When a field or document is empty, we use [blank], never the literal translation ~white~.

~If you leave the field in blank~ is not valid because 'in blank' is a literal translation of 'en blanco'. In English, use the adjective directly: [leave the field blank].

cards.sideA: Los correos se quedan en la cola de notificaciones porque el motor de envío no está corriendo en Blue.

sideB.text: Emails stay in the notification queue because the delivery engine is not running in Blue.
The emails remain in the notification queue since the delivery engine isn't running in Blue.
Emails get stuck in the notification queue because the sending engine is not running in Blue.
Emails stay in the notification queue because the sending engine is not running in Blue.
Emails are stuck waiting to be sent because the delivery engine isn't running in Blue.
Emails remain in the outbox because the sending engine is down in Blue.
The emails are piling up because the delivery service isn't active in Blue.

cards.note: A [queue] is a sequence or line of items waiting their turn to be processed.

cards.sideA: Es una restricción estándar en desarrollo para evitar el envío accidental de correos de prueba a usuarios reales.

sideB.text: It's a standard restriction in Dev to prevent accidental test emails from being sent to real users.
It's a standard development restriction to avoid sending test emails to real users by accident.
This is a standard restriction in development to prevent test emails from accidentally going out to real users.
It's a standard restriction in Dev to prevent accidental sends of emails to real users.
It's a standard restriction in Dev to prevent accidental sending of emails to real users.

cards.note: ~...prevent accidental sents of emails...~ is not valid because 'sents' is the past tense verb, not a noun. Use the gerund [sending] or the plural noun [sends].

cards.sideA: En Purple el servicio está en estado 'RUNNING', por lo que los correos deberían llegar sin problemas.

sideB.text: In Purple, the service is in 'RUNNING' status, so emails should arrive without any issues.
The service is in 'RUNNING' status in Purple, so the emails should go through fine.
In Purple, the service status is 'RUNNING', therefore emails should arrive seamlessly.
In Purple, the service is in the state of 'RUNNING', so emails should arrive without any issues.
In Purple, the service is in 'RUNNING' state, so emails should arrive without any issues.

cards.sideA: El flujo completo se puede validar en Purple ya que el Mailer de Workflow está activo allá.

sideB.text: The full workflow can be validated in Purple since the Workflow Mailer is active there.
The entire flow can be tested in Purple because the Workflow Mailer is enabled there.
We can validate the complete flow in Purple as the Workflow Mailer is active over there.

cards.note:
**[82]**

~The entire flow can be tested in Purple because of the Workflow Mailer is enabled there~ is not valid. Use [because] when followed by a full clause (subject + verb), and use [because of] when followed only by a noun phrase.
